title: Beyond The Barrel How Reserves Risk And Production Shape Oil Trade
section_path: Beyond The Barrel How Reserves Risk And Production Shape Oil Trade
section_path_text: Beyond The Barrel How Reserves Risk And Production Shape Oil Trade
summary:
Production
In energy economics, proven oil reserves refer to quantities of crude oil that geological and engineering data demonstrate with reasonable certainty to be recoverable under existing economic and operational conditions. Reserves should not be confused with production. While reserves represent potential, production refers to actual extraction volumes brought to market.
